resources.string:
Ingredients:
2 lbs pork (shoulder or thin slices)
3 dried guajillo chiles (or chili powder substitute)
2 cloves garlic
1/4 cup pineapple juice
2 tbsp vinegar
1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp oregano
1/2 pineapple (chunks)
Corn tortillas
Onion + cilantro
Steps:
Make the marinade: Blend soaked guajillo chiles, garlic, pineapple juice, vinegar, and spices.
Marinate the pork: Coat pork and let sit at least 2 hours (overnight is best).
Cook the meat: Pan-fry or grill until slightly crispy on edges.
Add pineapple: Toss pineapple chunks into the pan for the last 2–3 minutes.
Assemble tacos: Warm tortillas → add pork → top with pineapple, onion, cilantro.

2 cups cooked shredded chicken
8 corn tortillas
2 cups enchilada sauce (store-bought or homemade)
1–2 cups shredded cheese
Sour cream (optional) Steps:
Prep oven: Preheat to 375°F (190°C).
Soften tortillas: Lightly fry or microwave so they don’t crack.
Fill tortillas: Add chicken + a little cheese, then roll them.
Place in dish: Put rolled tortillas seam-side down in a baking dish.
Add sauce + cheese: Pour enchilada sauce over the top and sprinkle cheese.
Bake: Bake for 20 minutes until bubbly.
Serve: Add sour cream or cilantro if you want.

Ingredients:
3 ripe avocados
1 lime (juice)
1/4 onion (finely chopped)
1 tomato (diced)
2 tbsp cilantro
Salt to taste
Steps:
Mash avocados: Use a fork—leave it slightly chunky.
Add lime + salt: Mix immediately to prevent browning.
Mix in veggies: Add onion, tomato, cilantro.
Taste and adjust: Add more salt or lime if needed.
Serve fresh: Best eaten right away with chips or tacos.

2 lbs pork (shoulder or ribs)
1 large can hominy (drained)
3 dried guajillo chiles
2 cloves garlic
1 tsp oregano
Salt Toppings:
Shredded cabbage
Radish slices
Lime wedges Cook pork: Boil pork in a large pot with salt for 1–1.5 hours until tender.
Make chili sauce: Soak chiles → blend with garlic and some broth.
Strain sauce: Pour into pot with pork.
Add hominy: Stir in and simmer another 30 minutes.
Season: Add oregano and adjust salt.
Serve: Bowl it up and add toppings (cabbage, radish, lime).

4 ears of corn
1/4 cup mayo
1/4 cup sour cream
1/2 cup cotija cheese (or parmesan)
Chili powder
Lime wedges Steps:
Cook corn: Grill (best) or boil until tender.
Mix sauce: Combine mayo + sour cream.
Coat corn: Brush corn with the creamy mixture.
Add toppings: Sprinkle cheese + chili powder.
Finish with lime: Squeeze lime juice on top.
